quality.rationale: 基于出射爱丁顿-芬克尔斯坦坐标的多步推导结合黑洞视界附近红移物理图像

origin.question: [文件 1: 1_s15_15.10.md]Problem 15.10. A radio commentator is describing his radial fall into a Schwarzschild black hole. Just before he crosses the Schwarzschild radius his broadcast frequency starts becoming redshifted enormously with a time dependence exp $(-t / \text{constant})$ , where $t$ measures proper time at infinity. From the constant deduce the mass of the hole.## Solution
origin.solution: Solution 15.10. It is convenient to work in "outgoing Eddington-Finkelstein coordinates": Define $\mathbf {r}^{*} $ by $\mathrm {dr}^{*} / \mathrm {dr} = (1 - 2\mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r})^{-1}$ , that is$$\mathbf {r}^{*} = \mathbf {r} + 2 \mathbf {M} \log (\mathbf {r} / 2 \mathbf {M} - 1) + \text {c o n s t a n t}$$and define a retarded time coordinate$$\mathbf {u} = \mathbf {t} - \mathbf {r}^{*}$$The Schwarzschild metric in these coordinates is$$\mathrm {d s}^{2} = - (1 - 2 \mathrm {M} / \mathrm {r}) \mathrm {d u}^{2} - 2 \mathrm {d u d r} + \mathrm {r}^{2} \mathrm {d}\Omega^{2}. \tag {1}$$Putting $\mathrm {ds} = 0 $ , we see that outgoing radial photons travel along lines $\mathbf{u} =$ constant. The redshift is calculated by comparing the time between reception of photons and emission of photons:$$\frac {\lambda_{\inf ty}}{\lambda_{\text {e m i t t e r}}} = \frac {(\Delta t)_{\inf ty}}{(\Delta \tau)_{\text {e m}}} = \frac {(\Delta u)_{\inf ty}}{(\Delta \tau)_{\text {e m}}} = \left. \frac {(\Delta u)_{\text {e m}}}{(\Delta \tau)_{\text {e m}}} = \frac {\mathrm {d} u}{\mathrm {d} \tau}\right|_{\text {e m}}. \tag {2}$$Note how we can relate times at infinity to times at the emitter by using $u =$ constant along the rays.We must therefore find the component $\mathrm {d} u / \mathrm {d}\tau = \mathbf{U}^{\mathbf {u}}$ of the emitter's 4-velocity $\mathbf{U}$ as a function of t.Now u and t are both ignorable coordinates and$$\mathrm {U}_{\mathrm {t}} = \mathrm {U}_{\mathrm {u}} = \text {c o n s t a n t} = - \tilde {\mathrm {E}} \tag {3}$$and $\mathbf{U}\cdot \mathbf{U} = -\mathbf{1}$ implies![](images/cd41dda0fa8b84e6846d5afb92b49f93edb7e171ebda80df7eebe73c610b9a3f.jpg)$$U_{r} = \frac {- \tilde {E} - \left(\tilde {E}^{2} - 1 + 2 M / r\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}}{1 - 2 M / r}, \tag {4}$$therefore$$\mathbf {U}^{\mathrm {u}} = \mathbf {g}^{\mathrm {uu}} \mathbf {U}_{\mathrm {u}} + \mathbf {g}^{\mathrm {ur}} \mathbf {U}_{r} = 0 + \left[ \tilde {\mathbf {E}} + \left(\tilde {\mathbf {E}}^{2} - 1 + 2 \mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}\right] (1 - 2 \mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r})^{- 1} \qquad (5)$$and$$\begin{array}{l}\mathbf {U}^{\mathrm {r}} = \mathbf {g}^{\mathrm {ur}} \mathbf {U}_{\mathrm {u}} + \mathbf {g}^{\mathrm {rr}} \mathbf {U}_{\mathrm {r}} = \tilde {\mathbf {E}} + \left[ - \tilde {\mathbf {E}} - \left(\tilde {\mathbf {E}}^{2} - 1 + 2 \mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}\right] \\= - \left(\tilde {\mathrm {E}}^{2} - 1 + 2 \mathrm {M} / \mathrm {r}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}. \tag {6} \\\end{array}$$Thus$$\frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {r}}{\mathrm {d} u} = \frac {\mathbf {U}^{r}}{\mathbf {U}^{u}} = \frac {- \left(\tilde {\mathbf {E}}^{2} - 1 + 2 \mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} (1 - 2 \mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r})}{\tilde {\mathbf {E}} + \left(\tilde {\mathbf {E}}^{2} - 1 + 2 \mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}} \tag {7}$$Near $\mathbf{r} = 2\mathbf{M}$ , Equation (7) gives$$\begin{array}{l}\mathrm {d} \mathbf {u} \approx - 2 (1 - 2 \mathrm {M} / \mathrm {r})^{- 1} \mathrm {d} \mathbf {r} \approx - 2 (\mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M} - 1)^{- 1} \mathrm {d} \mathbf {r} \\u \approx - 4 M \log (r / 2 M - 1) + c o n s t a n t \\1 - 2 \mathrm {M} / \mathrm {r} \sim \exp (- \mathrm {u} / 4 \mathrm {M}). \\\end{array}$$Thus Equation (5) implies that, as $\mathbf{r} \to 2\mathbf{M}$$$U^{u} \sim e^{+ u / 4 M}.$$But $\mathbf{u} = \mathbf {t} +$ constant for an observer at fixed (large) $\mathbf {r} $ , so finally$$\lambda_{\text {e m i t t e r}} / \lambda_{\inf ty} \sim \exp (- t / 4 M).$$

killing_vectors.name: time_translation
killing_vectors.description: 时间平移 Killing 矢量 (∂_t)，对应能量守恒

killing_vectors.name: rot_phi
killing_vectors.description: 绕 φ 轴旋转 Killing 矢量 (∂_φ)，对应角动量守恒
